text: Inject session relaunch prompts for the agent on natural halts
description: During reflections or idle states, warn the agent that the wrapper code is dirty and instruct it to conclude its task and issue the relaunch command.

reflections.context: Second step of the self-rebuild cluster (5af61b3e), consuming the wrapperDirty flag from 4a96fff0: at a natural halt, tell the worker its own wrapper edits won't take effect until a rebuild+relaunch.
reflections.outcome: Added buildWrapperRelaunchPrompt() (pure) and WatchdogService.maybeWarnWrapperDirty() (latched, async post to worker), wired into the idle-escalation branch of tick() so it fires at a natural halt and yields the turn. Warns once, lists changed files, and instructs commit-then-relaunch, referencing the broker's existing verify gate.
reflections.effectiveness: Verified-done: `tsc --noEmit` clean and common suite 55/55 (3 new tests) — builder lists files + explains relaunch, truncates long lists (+N more), and maybeWarnWrapperDirty posts to the worker exactly once (no re-warn on the next halt).
reflections.lesson: post() bails when !enabled and auto-prepends WORKER_SUPERVISOR_PREFIX to non-slash worker messages — tests must set enabled=true and assert on substrings. Kept the prompt builder pure so its wording is unit-testable without driving the async post/timer path.
reflections.pattern: Latch one-shot interjections (sent-once flag) so supervisor guidance isn't drowned out by repeated halts; reuse the existing detection seam rather than re-scanning.

status.comment: WatchdogService.maybeWarnWrapperDirty() injects a one-time (latched) warning to the worker at the natural-halt branch of tick() when wrapperDirty (from sibling 4a96fff0). Pure builder buildWrapperRelaunchPrompt() lists changed files and instructs: reach a COMMITTED stopping point, then relaunch to deploy (references the existing broker.relaunch verify gate). Yields the turn after warning. The automated relaunch trigger (indicator file) remains 29021d9a. 3 new tests; common suite 55/55 + type-check clean.
